title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
description: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

title: OpenAI Executive Kevin Weil Is Leaving the Company
description: The former Instagram VP is departing the ChatGPT-maker, which is folding the AI science application he led into Codex.

description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

description: Data centers face construction delays and energy bottleneck as resistance grows.

title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
description: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto plants. title: Musk v. Altman Is a Battle for OpenAI’s Soul
description: In Musk v. Altman, a jury will soon determine whether OpenAI has strayed from its founding mission to ensure AGI benefits humanity. Here’s what to know.

title: The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
description: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.
